import_jobs.contributes: CVE records, descriptions, CVSS scores, CPE applicability
Known Exploited Vulnerabilities and ransomware flags

results.asset: PHP
results.asset_raw: PHP
results.kev_desc: PHP, specifically Windows-based PHP used in CGI mode, contains an OS command injection vulnerability that allows for arbitrary code execution. This vulnerability is a patch bypass for CVE-2012-1823.
results.description: In PHP versions 8.1.* before 8.1.29, 8.2.* before 8.2.20, 8.3.* before 8.3.8, when using Apache and PHP-CGI on Windows, if the system is set up to use certain code pages, Windows may use "Best-Fit" behavior to replace characters in command line given to Win32 API functions. PHP CGI module may misinterpret those characters as PHP options, which may allow a malicious user to pass options to PHP binary being run, and thus reveal the source code of scripts, run arbitrary PHP code on the server, etc.
results.action: Patch immediately: actively exploited in ransomware campaigns.
results.summary: CVE-2024-4577 affects PHP and carries a critical severity rating (CVSS 9.8). EPSS puts exploitation at a high probability (94.4 percent in the next 30 days, in the top 1 percent of all CVEs by exploitation probability), and CISA confirms active exploitation in ransomware campaigns.

results.asset: Fortinet FortiOS
results.asset_raw: Fortinet FortiOS
results.kev_desc: Fortinet FortiOS contains an out-of-bound write vulnerability that allows a remote unauthenticated attacker to execute code or commands via specially crafted HTTP requests.
results.description: A out-of-bounds write in Fortinet FortiOS versions 7.4.0 through 7.4.2, 7.2.0 through 7.2.6, 7.0.0 through 7.0.13, 6.4.0 through 6.4.14, 6.2.0 through 6.2.15, 6.0.0 through 6.0.17, FortiProxy versions 7.4.0 through 7.4.2, 7.2.0 through 7.2.8, 7.0.0 through 7.0.14, 2.0.0 through 2.0.13, 1.2.0 through 1.2.13, 1.1.0 through 1.1.6, 1.0.0 through 1.0.7 allows attacker to execute unauthorized code or commands via specifically crafted requests
results.action: Patch immediately: actively exploited in ransomware campaigns.
results.summary: CVE-2024-21762 affects Fortinet FortiOS and carries a critical severity rating (CVSS 9.8). EPSS puts exploitation at a high probability (92.5 percent in the next 30 days, in the top 1 percent of all CVEs by exploitation probability), and CISA confirms active exploitation in ransomware campaigns.

results.asset: VMware vSphere
results.asset_raw: VMware vSphere
results.kev_desc: VMware ESXi contains an authentication bypass vulnerability. A malicious actor with sufficient Active Directory (AD) permissions can gain full access to an ESXi host that was previously configured to use AD for user management by re-creating the configured AD group ('ESXi Admins' by default) after it was deleted from AD.
results.description: VMware ESXi contains an authentication bypass vulnerability. A malicious actor with sufficient Active Directory (AD) permissions can gain full access to an ESXi host that was previously  configured to use AD for user management https://blogs.vmware.com/vsphere/2012/09/joining-vsphere-hosts-to-active-directory.html  by re-creating the configured AD group ('ESXi Admins' by default) after it was deleted from AD.
results.action: Patch immediately: actively exploited in ransomware campaigns.
results.summary: CVE-2024-37085 affects VMware vSphere and carries a medium severity rating (CVSS 6.8). EPSS puts exploitation at a high probability (79.1 percent in the next 30 days, higher than 99 percent of all CVEs), and CISA confirms active exploitation in ransomware campaigns.

results.asset: Mozilla Firefox
results.asset_raw: Mozilla Firefox
results.kev_desc: Mozilla Firefox and Firefox ESR contain a use-after-free vulnerability in Animation timelines that allows for code execution in the content process.
results.description: An attacker was able to achieve code execution in the content process by exploiting a use-after-free in Animation timelines. We have had reports of this vulnerability being exploited in the wild. This vulnerability affects Firefox < 131.0.2, Firefox ESR < 128.3.1, Firefox ESR < 115.16.1, Thunderbird < 131.0.1, Thunderbird < 128.3.1, and Thunderbird < 115.16.0.
results.action: Patch immediately: actively exploited in ransomware campaigns.
results.summary: CVE-2024-9680 affects Mozilla Firefox and carries a critical severity rating (CVSS 9.8). EPSS puts exploitation at a moderate probability (30.8 percent in the next 30 days, higher than 97 percent of all CVEs), and CISA confirms active exploitation in ransomware campaigns.

results.asset: VMware vSphere
results.asset_raw: VMware vSphere
results.kev_desc: VMware ESXi contains an arbitrary write vulnerability. Successful exploitation allows an attacker with privileges within the VMX process to trigger an arbitrary kernel write leading to an escape of the sandbox.
results.description: VMware ESXi contains an arbitrary write vulnerability. A malicious actor with privileges within the VMX process may trigger an arbitrary kernel write leading to an escape of the sandbox.
results.action: Patch immediately: actively exploited in ransomware campaigns.
results.summary: CVE-2025-22225 affects VMware vSphere and carries a high severity rating (CVSS 8.2). EPSS puts exploitation at a low probability (9.8 percent in the next 30 days, higher than 93 percent of all CVEs), and CISA confirms active exploitation in ransomware campaigns.

results.asset: Microsoft 365 Apps
results.asset_raw: Microsoft 365 Apps for Business
results.kev_desc: Microsoft Outlook contains an improper input validation vulnerability that allows for remote code execution. Successful exploitation of this vulnerability would allow an attacker to bypass the Office Protected View and open in editing mode rather than protected mode.
results.description: Microsoft Outlook Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
results.action: Patch immediately: confirmed exploited in the wild (CISA KEV).
results.summary: CVE-2024-21413 affects Microsoft 365 Apps and carries a critical severity rating (CVSS 9.8). EPSS puts exploitation at a high probability (93.0 percent in the next 30 days, in the top 1 percent of all CVEs by exploitation probability), and CISA confirms it is being exploited in the wild.

results.asset: Apache HTTP Server
results.asset_raw: Apache HTTP Server
results.kev_desc: Apache HTTP Server contains an improper escaping of output vulnerability in mod_rewrite that allows an attacker to map URLs to filesystem locations that are permitted to be served by the server but are not intentionally/directly reachable by any URL, resulting in code execution or source code disclosure.
results.description:
Improper escaping of output in mod_rewrite in Apache HTTP Server 2.4.59 and earlier allows an attacker to map URLs to filesystem locations that are permitted to be served by the server but are not intentionally/directly reachable by any URL, resulting in code execution or source code disclosure. 

Substitutions in server context that use a backreferences or variables as the first segment of the substitution are affected.  Some unsafe RewiteRules will be broken by this change and the rewrite flag "UnsafePrefixStat" can be used to opt back in once ensuring the substitution is appropriately constrained.
results.action: Patch immediately: confirmed exploited in the wild (CISA KEV).
results.summary: CVE-2024-38475 affects Apache HTTP Server and carries a critical severity rating (CVSS 9.1). EPSS puts exploitation at a high probability (93.9 percent in the next 30 days, in the top 1 percent of all CVEs by exploitation probability), and CISA confirms it is being exploited in the wild.

results.asset: VMware vSphere
results.asset_raw: VMware vSphere
results.kev_desc: Broadcom VMware vCenter Server contains an out-of-bounds write vulnerability in the implementation of the DCERPC protocol. This could allow a malicious actor with network access to vCenter Server to send specially crafted network packets, potentially leading to remote code execution.
results.description: vCenter Server contains a heap-overflow vulnerability in the implementation of the DCERPC protocol. A malicious actor with network access to vCenter Server may trigger this vulnerability by sending a specially crafted network packet potentially leading to remote code execution.
results.action: Patch immediately: confirmed exploited in the wild (CISA KEV).
results.summary: CVE-2024-37079 affects VMware vSphere and carries a critical severity rating (CVSS 9.8). EPSS puts exploitation at a high probability (82.3 percent in the next 30 days, higher than 99 percent of all CVEs), and CISA confirms it is being exploited in the wild.

results.asset: Fortinet FortiOS
results.asset_raw: Fortinet FortiOS
results.kev_desc: Fortinet FortiOS, FortiPAM, FortiProxy, and FortiWeb contain a format string vulnerability that allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to execute arbitrary code or commands via specially crafted requests.
results.description: A use of externally-controlled format string in Fortinet FortiOS versions 7.4.0 through 7.4.2, 7.2.0 through 7.2.6, 7.0.0 through 7.0.13, FortiProxy versions 7.4.0 through 7.4.2, 7.2.0 through 7.2.8, 7.0.0 through 7.0.14, FortiPAM versions 1.2.0, 1.1.0 through 1.1.2, 1.0.0 through 1.0.3, FortiSwitchManager versions 7.2.0 through 7.2.3, 7.0.0 through 7.0.3 allows attacker to execute unauthorized code or commands via specially crafted packets.
results.action: Patch immediately: confirmed exploited in the wild (CISA KEV).

results.asset: VMware vSphere
results.asset_raw: VMware vSphere
results.kev_desc: VMware ESXi and Workstation contain a time-of-check time-of-use (TOCTOU) race condition vulnerability that leads to an out-of-bounds write. Successful exploitation enables an attacker with local administrative privileges on a virtual machine to execute code as the virtual machine's VMX process running on the host.
results.description: VMware ESXi, and Workstation contain a TOCTOU (Time-of-Check Time-of-Use) vulnerability that leads to an out-of-bounds write. A malicious actor with local administrative privileges on a virtual machine may exploit this issue to execute code as the virtual machine's VMX process running on the host.
results.action: Patch immediately: confirmed exploited in the wild (CISA KEV).
results.summary: CVE-2025-22224 affects VMware vSphere and carries a critical severity rating (CVSS 9.3). EPSS puts exploitation at a moderate probability (47.4 percent in the next 30 days, higher than 98 percent of all CVEs), and CISA confirms it is being exploited in the wild.

results.asset: Google Chrome
results.asset_raw: Google Chrome
results.kev_desc: Google Chromium Mojo on Windows contains a sandbox escape vulnerability caused by a logic error, which results from an incorrect handle being provided in unspecified circumstances. This vulnerability could affect multiple web browsers that utilize Chromium, including, but not limited to, Google Chrome, Microsoft Edge, and Opera.
results.description: Incorrect handle provided in unspecified circumstances in Mojo in Google Chrome on Windows prior to 134.0.6998.177 allowed a remote attacker to perform a sandbox escape via a malicious file. (Chromium security severity: High)
results.action: Patch immediately: confirmed exploited in the wild (CISA KEV).
results.summary: CVE-2025-2783 affects Google Chrome and carries a high severity rating (CVSS 8.3). EPSS puts exploitation at a moderate probability (46.9 percent in the next 30 days, higher than 98 percent of all CVEs), and CISA confirms it is being exploited in the wild.
